case_title: 20 PARKRIDGE TIC VS. 20 PARKRIDGE LLC et al

docket_entries.description: SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF 20 PARKRIDGE TIC SERVED JUN-24-2014, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT LHJS INVESTMENTS LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS 20 PARKRIDGE LLC, LHJS INVESTMENTS LLC, MAGNATE FUND #2 AND JOHN SIMONSE'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY ACTION PENDING SUCH ARBITRATION (CCP 1281.4) - THE PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY ACTION PENDING SUCH ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ASSERTS CLAIMS FOR BREACH OF THE PURCHASE AGREEMENT ("PA") (7TH CAUSE OF ACTION); BREACH OF THE TENANCY IN COMMON AGREEMENT ("TICA") (8TH AND 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION); BREACH OF CONTRACTS BETWEEN TENANTS IN COMMON AND DEFENDANTS AS TO WHICH PLAINTIFF ASSERTS IT IS A THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY (6TH CAUSE OF ACTION); AND CLAIMS THAT ARE INEXTRICABLY INTERTWINED WITH THESE AGREEMENTS. PLAINTIFF SEEKS DAMAGES ON BEHALF OF INDIVIDUAL OWNERS/TENANTS IN COMMON. THE PA AND THE TICA BOTH CONTAIN BROAD ARBITRATION CLAUSES THAT ENCOMPASS PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. "A NONSIGNATORY PLAINTIFF CAN BE COMPELLED TO ARBITRATE A CLAIM EVEN AGAINST A NONSIGNATORY DEFENDANT, WHEN THE CLAIM IS ITSELF BASED ON, OR INEXTRICABLY INTERTWINED WITH THE CONTRACT CONTAINING THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE." JSM TUSCANY, LLC V. SUPERIOR COURT, 193 CAL. APP. 4TH 122, 1241 (2011). DEFENDANT LEHANE HAS NOT BEEN SERVED AND IS NOT A MOVING DEFENDANT ON THIS PETITION. THE PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE MOVING DEFENDANTS. THE MOTION TO STAY ACTION PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF THE ARBITRATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE MOVING DEFENDANTS AND ANY CLAIMS AGAINST LEHANE. (SEE CCP 1281.4.) THE COURT IS RELYING ON THE VERSION OF THE TICA THAT IS ATTACHED TO THE DECLARATION OF JAMES DUCLAIR IN OPPOSITION TO THE PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, AS EXH. A. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/MJM

Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action Pending Such Arbitration (Ccp 1281.4)
Off Calendar Sep-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL T. KENNEDY JR. OF KASDAN WEBER TURNER LLP, TEL. NO. (925) 906-9220 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. GLEN H. WECHSLER, TEL. NO. (925) 274-0200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS 20 PARKRIDGE LLC, LHJS INVESTMENTS LLC, MAGNATE FUND #2 and JOHN SIMONSE'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action Pending Such Arbitration (CCP 1281.4) - The Petition to Compel Arbitration and to Stay Action pending such arbitration is granted. Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint asserts claims for breach of the Purchase Agreement ("PA") (7th cause of action); breach of the Tenancy in Common Agreement ("TICA") (8th and 9th causes of action); breach of contracts between tenants in common and Defendants as to which Plaintiff asserts it is a third party beneficiary (6th cause of action); and claims that are inextricably intertwined with these agreements. Plaintiff seeks damages on behalf of individual owners/tenants in common. The PA and the TICA both contain broad arbitration clauses that encompass Plaintiff's claims. "A nonsignatory plaintiff can be compelled to arbitrate a claim even against a nonsignatory defendant, when the claim is itself based on, or inextricably intertwined with the contract containing the arbitration clause." JSM Tuscany, LLC v. Superior Court, 193 Cal. App. 4th 122, 1241 (2011). Defendant Lehane has not been served and is not a moving defendant on this petition. The Petition to Compel Arbitration is granted as to the moving defendants. The motion to stay action pending the conclusion of the arbitration is granted as to the moving defendants and any claims against Lehane. (See CCP 1281.4.) The court is relying on the version of the TICA that is attached to the Declaration of James Duclair in opposition to the petition to compel arbitration, as Exh. A. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/MJM
